channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: 'AI can be relentless in a way that a human is not': With too few experts to go around, this startup is chasing a Matrix-style idea of “copying and pasting” expertise
item.description: With too few human experts, this AI startup is chasing a Matrix-style idea of “copying and pasting” expertise.

item.title: Disney Just Locked in Its First Summer 2028 Blockbusters
item.description: 'Lilo & Stitch' and 'Incredibles' sequels are coming back to the big screen within weeks of each other.

item.title: VPN deal of the week: There’s just 5 days left to bag a free 12-month Calm Premium subscription with Surfshark
item.description: Improve your sleep and your security in one, easy subscription

item.title: Prime Video's Scarpetta invites viewers to solve a gripping case — but butchering the source material is the show's worst crime of all
item.description: Scarpetta is Prime Video's take on an iconic Patricia Cornwell character but it's nothing like the books.

item.title: 'Further ⁠proceedings might not be necessary': Elon Musk and SEC in talks to settle Twitter deal lawsuit
item.description: Elon Musk and the SEC want to settle this lawsuit outside of court, and have asked for a two-week extension to reach an agreement.

item.title: Ligue 1 et IPTV illégale : des utilisateurs condamnés à des amendes en France
item.description: La Ligue de football professionnel (LFP) monte le ton d'un cran dans sa lutte contre l'IPTV illégale. Cette fois, elle a ciblé des utilisateurs de ces services, qui ont été condamnés à des amendes jusqu'à 400 €. Une décision de justice symbolique certes, mais qui ouvre la voie à d'autres condamnations.

item.title: Hubble catches rare view of a comet crumbling
item.description: NASA and ESA have released new images from the Hubble Space Telescope of a comet breaking up as it exits the solar system, captured as part of study recently published in the journal Icarus. The images are notable not only because they offer a more detailed view of the inside of a comet, which could offer new information about the early days of the universe, but also because they were taken by accident.Photographing K1, or "Comet C/2025 K1" as it's officially known, wasn't the original intention of the study. "This comet [was] observed because our original comet was not viewable due to some ne...

item.title: Un VPN gratuit et du split-screen : Firefox 149 est la version de la rédemption
item.description: Mozilla a visiblement entendu les sifflets des derniers mois. Après avoir tenté de nous imposer de l'IA à chaque coin de fenêtre, Firefox 149 revient aux fondamentaux.

item.title: Trump’s Embrace of AI Is Blowing Up the Whole Reason He Wanted Tariffs in the First Place
item.description: Who could have seen this coming?

item.title: New MiniMax M2.7 proprietary AI model is 'self-evolving' and can perform 30-50% of reinforcement learning research workflow
item.description: In the last few years, Chinese AI startup MiniMax has become one of the most exciting in the crowded global AI marketplace, carving out a reputation for delivering frontier-level large language models (LLMs) with open source licenses and before that, high-quality AI video generation models (Hailuo). The release of MiniMax M2.7 today — a new proprietary LLM designed to perform well powering AI agents and as the backend to third-party harnesses and tools like Claude Code, Kilo Code and OpenClaw — marks yet a new milestone: Rather than relying solely on human-led fine-tuning, MiniMax has leverag...

item.title: Trump’s AI chief’s big Iran warning gets big time ignored
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, a newsletter for Verge subscribers about the politics of technology and the technology of politics - now landing in your inbox on Wednesdays! If someone has forwarded this email to you, and you're not a Verge subscriber yet, you should sign up right here, and not just because it would […]

item.title: J’ai essayé la Nio ET5 Touring électrique qui se « recharge » en 3 minutes : une expérience hors du commun
item.description: J'ai pu essayer en Chine la Nio ET5 Touring, la voiture électrique chinoise qui se « recharge » en 3 minutes grâce à l'échange de batterie, et avec une autonomie de 700 km. Voici mon avis.

item.title: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is now available for iPhones
item.description: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is now available as a standalone app for iPhone users. The tool initially debuted last summer on PCs, but cost $200 a month. The new app is free, as is the recently-released Android version. Comet is an AI-powered browser, which has become a hot product category throughout the past year or so. This is basically a web browser combined with a chatbot that can perform some tasks on a user's behalf. Like related tools, people can ask it to summarize a webpage or conduct additional research for more context about a subject. Comet is now available for iOS.Download on th...

item.title: Realme 16 Pro+ : ce nouveau smartphone doté d’une batterie XXL et d’un écran AMOLED à 144 Hz s’offre déjà une remise de 110 €
item.description: Avec le Realme 16 Pro+, la marque chinoise propose un smartphone qui mêle autonomie et puissance grâce à l'intégration du Snapdragon 7 Gen 4, absente sur la version Pro traditionnelle. Le tout tombe à 419 euros grâce aux codes ASFR60 ou FRAS60 disponibles chez AliExpress.

item.title: FBI is buying location data to track US citizens, director confirms
item.description: FBI director Kash Patel told lawmakers that the agency is actively purchasing commercially available location data, which can track Americans without needing a warrant.

item.title: Robinhood is making a social network
item.description: Robinhood is now beta testing a Twitter-like social media platform it calls Robinhood Social. The trading-focused platform was announced last September at the company's HOOD Summit, but it's rolling out to 1,000 people who attended the event and will expand to "an additional 10,000 customers over the coming weeks," according to Robinhood. On Robinhood Social, […]...

item.title: The new iPad Air M4 'remains the iPad most people should buy' — and it's already on sale at Walmart
item.description: The all-new iPad Air M4 is the best iPad for most people, and you can find the 11-inch tablet already discounted at Walmart.

item.title: Mars : la Nasa détecte sous la surface des structures qui relancent l’histoire de l’eau !
item.description: Grâce à ses équipements, le rover Perseverance a sondé les profondeurs du sous-sol martien et mis au jour des structures enfouies témoignant d’anciens systèmes fluviaux. Ces découvertes suggèrent que l’eau a façonné la région bien avant la formation du célèbre delta martien, renforçant...

item.title: ChatGPT did not cure a dog’s cancer
item.description: When an Australian tech entrepreneur with no background in biology or medicine said ChatGPT helped save his dog from cancer, the story spread with the kind of validation Big Tech has long craved: proof that AI will revolutionize medicine and take on one of its deadliest diseases. The reality, as usual, is more complicated. The […]

item.title: Exclusive: $599 MacBook Neo easily beats $1,119 Dell laptop on Windows 11 benchmarks - yes, macOS may well be the best platform ever for Microsoft's OS
item.description: Despite running a mobile CPU and Windows in a virtual machine, the MacBook laptop excelled in single-core CPU performance.

item.title: Apple’s MacBook Pro M5 laptop is massively discounted at Amazon - and it's a better choice for creators than the MacBook Neo
item.description: Forget the Apple Neo — the more powerful MacBook Pro with M5 chip is nearly $200 off right now on Amazon.

item.title: Why Walmart and OpenAI Are Shaking Up Their Agentic Shopping Deal
item.description: After OpenAI’s Instant Checkout feature fell short, Walmart is instead embedding its Sparky chatbot directly into ChatGPT and Google Gemini.

item.title: The Cast of ‘Dune: Part Three’ Tiptoed Around Its Biggest Mysteries
item.description: Jason Momoa and Florence Pugh are back and asking the 'Dune' questions fans want answered.

item.title: The US Grossly Undercounted Covid Deaths Early On, Updated Toll Shows
item.description: The official death toll of covid-19 between 2020 and 2021 was off by nearly 20%, new research finds.

item.title: What should you evolve Eevee into on Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness for Nintendo Switch 2? Here’s my opinion as a life-long Pokémon fan
item.description: Pokémon XD: Gale of Darkness has landed on Nintendo Switch 2 — here’s what you should evolve your Eevee into.

item.title: Which Amazon Echo Show should I buy? Here are the key differences to know
item.description: There are lots of Echo Show discounts right now — here's how to figure out which one you want.

item.title: Only 9% of global firms are ready to handle AI-driven threats, and many are dangerously overconfident
item.description: Constantly verifying user identities is the way forward - but many are unrealistic about their efforts.

item.title: Scientists tracked coffee drinkers for dementia risk over 43 years. Here’s what they found
item.description: A team of researchers investigated a previously inconclusive link between caffeinated coffee and tea with cognitive function. There’s long been debate as to whether coffee is good for you. But this new study suggests that caffeinated coffee, as well as caffeinated tea, could lead to lower incidence of dementia. So if your morning routine involves making a bleary-eyed beeline to the coffee maker immediately upon waking—you may be doing something right....

item.title: Northern lights tonight: Don’t miss your chance to catch a visible aurora borealis in 19 states. Here’s the forecast for where and when
item.description: NOAA is predicting a spectacular show thanks to a geomagnetic storm, above states from Washington to Michigan to Pennsylvania. The Northern Lights, also known as aurora borealis, may be visible in nearly 19 U.S. states tonight, Wednesday, March 18 into Thursday, March 19, according to the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s (NOAA) Space Weather Prediction Center....

item.title: Pardoned Nikola founder Trevor Milton is trying to raise $1B for AI-powered planes
item.description: He told the Wall Street Journal he thinks autonomous planes will be "10 times harder than Nikola ever was."

item.title: UK reverses course on AI copyright position after backlash
item.description: Chalk up a win for creative artists against AI companies. On Wednesday, the UK government abandoned its previous position on copyrighted works. It’s currently working on a data bill that, if unaltered, would have allowed AI companies like Google and OpenAI to train models on copyrighted materials without consent. Artists and other copyright holders would only have been offered a mere opt-out clause.After significant backlash, the UK backed off from that position. "We have listened," Technology Secretary Liz Kendall said on Wednesday. However, the government’s new stance is, well, not a stance ...

item.title: Si vous utilisez un clavier SwiftKey, nous avons une mauvaise nouvelle
item.description: Si vous utilisez SwiftKey pour sa prédiction redoutable, préparez-vous à passer à la caisse… en données. Microsoft vient d'annoncer que dès le 31 mai 2026, posséder un compte maison sera l'unique ticket d'entrée pour son clavier star sur Android et iOS.

item.title: Patreon CEO calls AI companies’ fair use argument ‘bogus,’ says creators should be paid
item.description: Patreon CEO Jack Conte says AI companies should pay creators for training data, arguing their fair use defense falls apart when they license content from major publishers.

item.title: Robot Losing Its Mind in a California Restaurant Is Just as Fed Up as Everyone Else
item.description: Dance like no one's watching.

item.title: This HP EliteBook laptop deal is massively discounted at Best Buy — but I found a mini PC with much better specs for far less
item.description: 14-inch HP EliteBook laptop gets a huge $2,360 price cut, but at $1,399, it’s still way more expensive than the Minisforum UM890 Pro mini PC with far better specs.

item.title: Mini PC : faut-il acheter maintenant avant l’augmentation des prix ? Voici deux conseils à suivre
item.description: La fête est finie pour les prix planchers sur les mini PC performants. Entre la demande folle pour l'IA et les pénuries de mémoire DDR5, Minisforum ajuste ses étiquettes vers le haut. Et c'est loin d'être le seul.

item.title: Microsoft will finally let you turn off Quick Resume for individual games
item.description: Microsoft is rolling out a number of Xbox Series X|S updates to those on its Xbox Insiders program, and one of them has been requested by the community for years. You’ll soon be able to disable the Quick Resume feature for specific games, meaning they'll boot up fresh each time you launch them. As a reminder, Quick Resume is a feature that Microsoft introduced with its current generation of consoles. It lets you suspend a number of games simultaneously so they’re running in the background even when the console is turned off. When you want to return to a game that supports Quick Resume, it'll d...

item.title: Traders flocked to prediction markets—now a criminal case is testing the model
item.description: The outcome could determine whether betting on real-world events remains a viable strategy. Kalshi made headlines last month over allowing people to place wagers on the Iran war. Now the prediction market platform is being sued by the state of Arizona, the first state to file criminal charges against the controversial company.

item.title: Kasa’s Matter-compatible smart plugs are on sale for $11 a pop
item.description: If you’re looking for an easy and affordable way to add smarts to your home, you can buy two Kasa Smart Wi-Fi Plug Slim smart plugs on sale for $21.99 ($8 off) at Amazon and B&H Photo. That’s the bundle’s best price in months and a good opportunity to upgrade things you already own instead […]

item.title: The Best Sonos Bluetooth Speaker Is $40 Off
item.description: Add the Sonos Roam 2 to your existing home setup, or take it to go with Bluetooth.

item.title: Surfshark boosts its ultimate security bundle with $1 million identity theft protection
item.description: Cybercrime is soaring, and Surfshark wants to foot the bill. The provider just added a massive $1 million identity theft insurance policy to its top-tier bundle for US users. Here is what you need to know.

item.title: The U.S. is temporarily waiving the Jones Act. Here’s what to know
item.description: The Jones Act mandates that ships carrying cargo and passengers between U.S. ports must be built in the U.S. and owned by Americans. As the U.S. and Israel’s war against Iran continues to upend energy markets and supply chains worldwide, the Trump administration says it will temporarily waive maritime shipping requirements under a more than century-old law known as the Jones Act....

item.title: My favorite robot vacuum now supports Matter
item.description: Matic, my current top pick for the best robot vacuum, just got a big update. The unique-looking floor bot now works with Google Home and Apple Home, with support coming through the smart home standard Matter. This enables voice control and smart home integrations, features that have been missing from the robot since launch. Now […]

item.title: The best AirPods deals you can get right now
item.description: If you know where to look, you can often score deals on Apple’s ever-expanding AirPods lineup. Both the first-gen AirPods Max and the AirPods 4 (with and without ANC) now consistently receive discounts, as do the newer AirPods Pro 3. And while major shopping events like Black Friday and Prime Day have delivered some of […]

item.title: The best boring (but essential) deals to shop during Amazons Big Spring Sale
item.description: From paper towels to dishwasher pods, here are the best early deals on boring but essential household goods ahead of Amazon's 2026 Big Spring Sale.

item.title: On a enfin trouvé le PC portable ultime pour les créatifs : test du Samsung Galaxy Book 6 Ultra
item.description: Samsung veut profiter des Intel Core Ultra 300 et de la génération RTX 50 pour faire revenir le Book 6 Ultra en France, à un tarif qui le fait entrer clairement dans le haut de gamme du géant coréen : voici le résultat.

item.title: Google Pixel 10a vs Pixel 10: which Pixel phone should you buy?
item.description: The Google Pixel 10a and Pixel 10 offer identical software and similar specs, but which mid-range Pixel phone should you buy?

item.title: PS5 Slim, trottinette premium Xiaomi et forfait 5G : les 3 bons plans tech à ne pas manquer ce mercredi 18 mars 2026
item.description: Ce mercredi 18 mars 2026, la sélection des meilleurs bons plans du jour fait la part belle aux produits qui font battre le cœur des technophiles.

item.title: ‘The people have been waiting!’ — GoPro teases a mysterious cinematic camera, which could have DJI and Insta360 worried
item.description: Following the announcement of a new, twice the power processor, GoPro has teased its next all-new kind of camera with cinematic shots

item.title: Rebel Audio is a new AI podcasting tool aimed at first-time creators
item.description: Rebel Audio is a new all-in-one podcasting tool that allows creators to record podcasts, edit, clip content for social, and publish episodes, all without ever leaving the platform.

item.title: The head of NASA, members of Congress, and Elon Musk want to make Pluto a planet again. Will Trump do it?
item.description: NASA administrator Jared Isaacman just urged the president to ‘make Pluto great again’—but Trump doesn’t have the power to do it alone. President Donald Trump first ran on a campaign to “make America great again.” Whether he’s been successful in doing so (or if America was ever great to begin with) is hotly contested—but even as the work continues on our home planet, one NASA leader is encouraging Trump to set his sights even further....

item.title: Double Fine’s Kiln pops out of the oven and onto PC, Xbox and PS5 on April 23
item.description: Double Fine is following up on Keeper — one of our favorite games of 2025 — with Kiln, a “multiplayer online pottery party brawler.” The game was showcased during Xbox’s Developer Direct stream in January and now it has a release date. It’s coming to Xbox Series X/S, Xbox on PC, Xbox Cloud, PlayStation 5 and Steam on April 23. Xbox said Kiln will be Handheld Optimized from the jump. In Kiln, you'll use a virtual pottery wheel to shape a piece of clay into a vessel and then decorate it. The size and shape will determine how much health you have, the water capacity of your vessel and your speed....

item.title: Spotify rolls out a huge Wear OS upgrade including new album art and handy tap gestures, giving you ‘even more control from your wrist’
item.description: Spotify's Wear OS upgrade is truly stunning, but its pretty new visuals are just the beginning.

item.title: 10+ Ninja kitchen deals to shop before the Amazon Big Spring Sale
item.description: Take up to 41% off Ninja Kitchen appliances like air fryers, blenders, and countertop ovens before the Amazon Big Spring Sale.

item.title: Frozen Food Item From Aldi Recalled Over ‘Rodent Hair’ Worries
item.description: Check your freezers.

item.title: Meta is pulling the plug on its VR metaverse and Meta Quest 3 fans don't know how to respond
item.description: Meta’s VR scale-back is heading into phase two as the company has revealed its timeline for the full closure of its Meta Horizon Worlds metaverse.
